inlineContent.text: is a low-level server interface for audio access. It is in charge of creating sample data (playable audio) as well as its playback via a voice interface.
This object emits the following signals:

abstract.text: Server interface for low-level audio access.
Returns the name of the bus with the index
Sets the name of the bus at index
Returns the relative time until the next mix occurs.
Name of the current device for audio input (see
). On systems with multiple audio inputs (such as analog, USB and HDMI audio), this can be used to select the audio input device. The value
will record audio on the system-wide default audio input. If an invalid device name is set, the value will be reverted back to
Returns the number of channels of the bus at index
Emitted when an audio bus is added, deleted, or moved.
Name of the current device for audio output (see
). On systems with multiple audio outputs (such as analog, USB and HDMI audio), this can be used to select the audio output device. The value
will play audio on the system-wide default audio output. If an invalid device name is set, the value will be reverted back to
Scales the rate at which audio is played (i.e. setting it to
will make the audio be played at half its speed).
